lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the East Side is pulling thirty-four — that's already above last year's pace at this point. Caroline, tell me the cold front's still moving in.
Northwest, 18 miles per hour, gusts to 28. It'll hit hard between 01:30 and 02:00. Temperature's dropping faster than predicted.

That's interesting because in 2159 — and I'm not saying this to be morbid — but the cold that year suppressed activity by 12 percent in hours five through seven. We might see the same pattern tonight.
Holden, I don't need you running the numbers on what the cold does to bodies. I need you to tell me if we're going to make it to dawn.
We always make it to dawn, Quinn. That's not the data point. The data point is whether anyone else does.
Wind's shifting north. That'll clear the fog off Mendota around four. Visibility will improve.

You know what I remember about this hour last year? I remember sitting in this chair thinking about Director Calloway locked in that Capitol building, and I thought — I actually thought — 'At least he knows where his next meal is coming from.' And then I poured number four.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 104 confirmed. {{ELAPSED}} into the night. East Side remains hottest zone — 34 kills, up 12% YoY through this hour.
Cold front arrival accelerated. Expect temperature drop of 8–12 degrees between 01:30–02:00. Exposure risk increasing for unsheltered populations.
Yahara River barricade (Willy Street) holding. No breaches reported. Neighborhood watch commander (identity unconfirmed) maintaining defensive posture.
